seoTitle: How to Add Tags in Zendesk: Manual and Automated
metaDescription: Learn how to add tags in Zendesk manually from the ticket sidebar, via macros, or through Admin Center business rules for consistent categorization.
intro: Untagged tickets are hard to sort, report on, or route automatically. This guide covers three ways to apply tags in Zendesk: typing them directly on a ticket, using macros to inject them automatically, and configuring admin-level field rules that generate tags from dropdown selections.

steps.heading: Find the tags field on a ticket
steps.body: Open an active support ticket and locate the Tags field in the left sidebar panel.

steps.heading: Add a tag manually
steps.body: Click into the tag input space, type your alphanumeric label, and press space or enter to apply it.

steps.heading: Use a macro shortcut
steps.body: Apply a saved macro that automatically injects pre-configured tag elements along with a canned response, saving repetitive typing.

steps.heading: Set up field-based tag rules
steps.body: In Admin Center, go to Objects and Rules and open the Tickets field rules to link dropdown options to auto-generated tags.

steps.heading: Follow formatting conventions
steps.body: Keep tags lowercase, use underscores instead of spaces, and avoid special characters so filters and reports don't break.

steps.heading: Audit your tag list
steps.body: Periodically check the Admin Center data management view to delete outdated tags or merge overlapping labels.

faq.question: Can I remove a tag from a ticket?
faq.answer: Yes, tags can be removed from the sidebar field the same way they're added, by editing the field directly.

faq.question: Do tags affect ticket routing?
faq.answer: Tags can be used as conditions in triggers and automations, so they can indirectly influence routing when combined with business rules.

faq.question: Why are some of my tags not filtering correctly?
faq.answer: Inconsistent formatting, like mixed case or special characters, is the most common cause of tags not matching in filters.

faq.question: Can end users see ticket tags?
faq.answer: No, tags are an internal organizational tool and are not visible to customers on the ticket.
